rationale: The cited paper is used in the citing paper's hypothesis-development logic, not just as a passing background reference. Salnikova et al. cite White et al. (2011) to support the claim that congruence between message framing and construal improves processing fluency/comprehension, and they use that premise to motivate their own congruent-frame predictions and process arguments. Although the citation is grouped with other studies and the citing paper does not reuse White et al.'s exact design, the cited work plays a meaningful conceptual role in the framing setup.
relation_description: The citing paper treats White et al. (2011) as conceptual prior evidence that frame-construal fit increases message effectiveness in environmental communication. It uses that fit principle as a basis for its own congruency hypotheses in a global-local identity and global brand messaging context.
